# QA Plan: Sprint 02 — Foundation + Core

**Date**: 2026-04-30
**Generated by**: `/qa-plan sprint`
**Scope**: 24 Must-Have stories across 7 systems (Foundation 13 stories + Core 11 stories)
**Engine**: Godot 4.6 (Forward+ Vulkan; GDScript; GdUnit4 v6.0.0 with 4.6.2 compat patch)
**Sprint File**: `production/sprints/sprint-02-foundation-core.md`
**Sprint Status File**: `production/sprint-status.yaml`

---

---

## Automated Tests Required

### SB-001 Events autoload — structural purity (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/events_purity_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0002 IG 2 + forbidden pattern `events_with_state_or_methods`):
- `events.gd` source contains zero `func ` declarations outside the `class_name` and `extends` header
- `events.gd` source contains zero `var ` declarations
- `events.gd` source contains zero `const ` declarations (other than as part of a signal type signature)
- The `Events` autoload appears at line 1 of `project.godot [autoload]` block per ADR-0007
- The `smoke_test_pulse` skeleton signal from Sprint 01 is removed
**Edge cases**: empty file body, header-only file, multi-line signal declarations
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### SB-002 Built-in-type signal declarations (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/events_signal_declarations_test.gd`
**What to test**: every signal declared on `events.gd` uses only built-in Godot types (StringName, int, float, bool, Vector3, Node, Resource, Array, Dictionary). No user-defined `class_name` types in payloads (per ADR-0002 IG 1). The MVP signal subset (8–12 signals listed in story) is present with the exact frozen signature.
**Edge cases**: enum-typed parameters (must be `int`, with the enum docstring referencing the owning class)
**Estimated test count**: ~10 (one per declared signal)

### SB-003 EventLogger debug autoload (Integration)
**Test file**: `tests/integration/foundation/event_logger_debug_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `EventLogger` autoload registers at line 2 per ADR-0007
- In debug build (`OS.is_debug_build()` true), `EventLogger` connects to all `Events` signals at `_ready` and prints emissions to stdout
- In release build path, `EventLogger._ready` exits early via `queue_free()` self-removal
- Emitting a signal with subscribers attached produces exactly one log line per emission
**Edge cases**: signal with no subscribers (no log line), signal emitted before `_ready` completes (no crash)
**Estimated test count**: ~4

### SB-004 Subscriber lifecycle + Node payload validity (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/subscriber_lifecycle_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0002 IG 3):
- Subscriber `_ready` connects with `Events.signal.connect(callable)` AND `is_connected` guard
- Subscriber `_exit_tree` disconnects with `is_connected` guard
- A test fixture node connecting twice does not produce duplicate connections (idempotent connect)
- Receiving a Node-typed payload after the publisher has been freed: subscriber detects via `is_instance_valid()` and skips handling without erroring
**Edge cases**: subscriber freed before publisher emits (no callback error); publisher freed mid-emit
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### SB-005 Anti-pattern enforcement — CI grep guards (Config/Data)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/anti_pattern_grep_test.gd`
**What to test**: a script-level test that runs grep against `src/core/signal_bus/events.gd` and adjacent files for the registered forbidden patterns (`events_with_state_or_methods`, `event_bus_enum_definition`, `wrapper_emit_methods`, `synchronous_request_response_through_bus`, `engine_signal_reemission`). Each pattern, if found, fails the test with a specific error message.
**Edge cases**: false-positive guard (e.g., a comment containing the word `func` should not trigger); patterns inside string literals
**Estimated test count**: ~5 (one per registered pattern)

### SB-006 Edge-case dispatch behavior (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/dispatch_edge_cases_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- No-dedup: emit the same signal twice in the same frame → both emissions delivered to subscribers
- Continue-on-error: subscriber A throws; subscribers B and C still receive the emission
- Subscriber order preserved across emissions (registration order)
- `Events.gd` signal emit cost is bounded — assert ≤ N microseconds for an N-subscriber emission (per ADR-0002 IG 5 perf bound)
**Edge cases**: 0 subscribers, 1 subscriber, 100 subscribers; subscriber that disconnects itself during callback
**Estimated test count**: ~6

### SL-001 SaveGame Resource + 7 typed sub-resources (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/save_game_resource_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0003 IG 11):
- `class_name SaveGame extends Resource` exists, has `FORMAT_VERSION = 2` const + `save_format_version` @export var sentinel
- Each of 7 sub-resources (`PlayerState`, `InventoryState`, `StealthAIState`, `CivilianAIState`, `DocumentCollectionState`, `MissionState`, `FailureRespawnState`) is a top-level file under `src/core/save_load/states/` with its own `class_name` (avoid the inner-class @export trap)
- `SaveGame` declares 7 typed `@export var` slots, one per sub-resource
**Edge cases**: missing sub-resource file → load test surfaces a clear error
**Estimated test count**: ~9 (1 SaveGame + 7 sub-resource shape tests + 1 inner-class trap test)

### SL-002 SaveLoadService autoload + atomic write (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/save_load_atomic_write_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0003 atomic-write protocol + Sprint 01 finding F1):
- `save_to_slot(slot, save_game)` writes to `slot_N.tmp.res` first, then `DirAccess.rename` to `slot_N.res`, then writes the metadata sidecar
- If the rename fails, the previous good `slot_N.res` is intact (not overwritten)
- `SaveLoad.FailureReason.RENAME_FAILED` is emitted via `save_failed` signal on rename failure
- `ResourceSaver.FLAG_COMPRESS` is applied
- `SaveLoadService` autoload registered at line 3 of `project.godot [autoload]`
**Edge cases**: write to a read-only path; disk-full simulation (mock `DirAccess`); concurrent saves to the same slot
**Estimated test count**: ~7

### SL-003 load_from_slot + type-guard + version-mismatch refusal (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/load_from_slot_type_guard_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0003 refuse-load-on-mismatch versioning):
- Loading a slot that doesn't exist emits `save_failed(SLOT_NOT_FOUND)`
- Loading a corrupted file (random bytes) → `is null` check OR `not is SaveGame` check fires → `save_failed(CORRUPT_FILE)`
- Loading a save with `save_format_version != FORMAT_VERSION` → `save_failed(VERSION_MISMATCH)`, the in-memory state is NOT mutated
- Successful load returns a valid `SaveGame` and emits `game_loaded(slot)`
**Edge cases**: file exists but is empty; file is correct format but missing a required sub-resource
**Estimated test count**: ~6

### SL-004 duplicate_deep state isolation (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/duplicate_deep_isolation_test.gd`
**What to test** (per ADR-0003 mandatory `duplicate_deep()` discipline on load):
- After load, mutating a top-level field of the in-memory `SaveGame` does NOT mutate the on-disk version (i.e., the load returned a deep copy, not a reference)
- Mutating a nested field inside a sub-resource (e.g., a `Dictionary[StringName, GuardRecord]` entry) does NOT mutate the on-disk version
- The forbidden pattern `forgotten_duplicate_deep_on_load` would be caught by an absent `.duplicate_deep()` call in `load_from_slot`
**Edge cases**: empty Dictionary, nested Array of Resources, cyclic reference (per ADR-0003 should be impossible by design — assert)
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### LOC-001 CSV registration + tr() runtime (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/localization_csv_tr_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `project.godot [internationalization]` block registers the localization CSV path
- Calling `tr("test.basic.key")` against a known fixture CSV returns the expected English string
- Calling `tr("nonexistent.key")` returns the key itself unmodified (Godot default fallback)
- `TranslationServer.set_locale("fr")` then `tr("test.basic.key")` returns the French row of the CSV
**Edge cases**: CSV row with embedded comma; CSV with BOM; missing locale column
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### LS-001 SectionRegistry + LSS autoload + fade overlay (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/section_registry_test.gd` + `tests/unit/foundation/lss_autoload_boot_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `SectionRegistry` Resource declares `sections: Dictionary[StringName, PackedScene]`, type-correct
- `LevelStreamingService` autoload boots, loads the registry resource without error, registers a fade overlay `CanvasLayer`
- Public API surfaces: `transition_to_section`, `reload_current_section`, `register_restore_callback`, `_simulate_registry_failure`, `evict_section_from_cache` (MVP no-op)
- Fade overlay starts hidden and is on a layer above gameplay
**Edge cases**: empty registry; missing scene reference in registry; registry file missing entirely (graceful fail with clear error)
**Estimated test count**: ~7

### LS-002 State machine + 13-step swap + signal emission (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/foundation/lss_state_machine_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- The 13-step happy-path forward swap executes in order (steps 1–13) — assert each step's observable outcome
- `section_entered(reason)` signal emits with the expected `TransitionReason` enum value (FORWARD, RESPAWN, LOAD)
- The state machine rejects re-entrant `transition_to_section` calls during an active transition
- After a successful swap, `current_section` reflects the new section ID
**Edge cases**: transition target = current section (no-op); transition called while autoload boot incomplete
**Estimated test count**: ~9

### IN-001 InputActions static class (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/input_actions_test.gd`
**What to test**: `InputActions.MOVE_FORWARD`, etc. are typed `StringName` constants; every declared action is registered in `project.godot [input]`; the constant value matches the action key.
**Edge cases**: an action declared but never bound; an action bound but not declared as a constant
**Estimated test count**: ~3

### IN-002 InputContext stack autoload (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/input_context_stack_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `InputContext` autoload boot
- `push(context)` / `pop()` follow LIFO order
- `current` returns the top of stack
- Pop on empty stack does not crash; returns a sentinel value
- Initial state is `GAMEPLAY`
**Edge cases**: pop more than push; push the same context twice (allowed; LIFO); push during another push callback (re-entrancy)
**Estimated test count**: ~6

### PC-001 PlayerCharacter scene root scaffold (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/player_character_scaffold_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `PlayerCharacter` scene root is `CharacterBody3D` with the correct collision layer + mask per ADR-0006
- Scene instantiates without error and adds itself to the SceneTree
- The scene exposes the documented public API surface as method stubs (return defaults; full implementation in PC-002+)
**Edge cases**: instantiation in headless mode (should not require a viewport)
**Estimated test count**: ~3

### PC-002 First-person camera + look input (Integration)
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/camera_look_input_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- Camera child node has `current = true` after PlayerCharacter `_ready`
- Mouse motion event with positive Y rotates camera pitch downward; clamp at `-89°` and `+89°`
- Mouse motion event with positive X rotates camera yaw left or right per the configured sensitivity
- Camera responds to `InputContext = GAMEPLAY` only; ignores look input when `InputContext = MENU`
**Edge cases**: extreme mouse delta (clamped); inverted-Y option (if implemented)
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### PC-003 Movement state machine + locomotion (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/movement_state_machine_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- WASD input transitions the state machine: IDLE → WALK
- Sprint modifier transitions WALK → SPRINT
- Crouch modifier transitions WALK → CROUCH (or SPRINT → CROUCH cancels sprint)
- Velocity vectors per state match the GDD-specified speeds (read from `design/gdd/player-character.md` §Formulas)
**Edge cases**: simultaneous opposing inputs (W+S → IDLE); sprint+crouch (crouch wins per pillar 3 stealth tone); transitioning while the input context is paused
**Estimated test count**: ~7

### PC-004 Noise perception surface (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/noise_perception_surface_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `get_noise_level()` returns the correct float per movement state (IDLE=0.0, WALK=mid, SPRINT=high) per GDD formula
- `get_silhouette_height()` returns the correct height per state (CROUCH = 0.5×, otherwise 1.0×)
- `global_transform.origin` is exposed (inherited from CharacterBody3D — assert direct-read access)
- The returned values are deterministic for the same input
**Edge cases**: state transition mid-frame (which state is reported?); emitter-time consistency between `get_noise_level()` and the `footstep_emitted.noise_radius_m` payload — the contract per ADR-0002 says they must mirror
**Estimated test count**: ~6

### PC-005 Interact raycast + query API (Integration)
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/interact_raycast_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `get_current_interact_target()` returns the StaticBody3D the camera-forward raycast hits within the configured range, on `LAYER_INTERACTABLES` per ADR-0006
- Returns `null` when nothing in range
- `is_hand_busy()` returns `true` while a stub busy-state is set (post-VS this'll integrate with Inventory; for VS it's a stub that always returns `false`)
- Interact action publishes `Events.player_interacted` payload with the target node reference
**Edge cases**: target hidden behind a wall; target on a different collision layer; target deleted between query and interact
**Estimated test count**: ~6

### FS-001 FootstepComponent scaffold (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/footstep_scaffold_test.gd`
**What to test**: the FootstepComponent attaches as a child of PlayerCharacter; reads PC movement state via direct property access; exposes a `cadence_seconds` typed-export.
**Edge cases**: parent is not a PlayerCharacter (assert + warn)
**Estimated test count**: ~3

### FS-002 Step cadence state machine (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/footstep_cadence_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- IDLE: no footstep timer
- WALK: timer fires every `walk_cadence_seconds`
- SPRINT: timer fires every `sprint_cadence_seconds` (faster than walk)
- CROUCH: timer fires every `crouch_cadence_seconds` (slower than walk)
- Cadence values per the GDD `design/gdd/footstep-component.md` §Formulas
**Edge cases**: rapid state transitions (timer should reset / re-base appropriately)
**Estimated test count**: ~5

### FS-003 Surface detection raycast (Logic)
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/footstep_surface_raycast_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- Downward raycast from the foot position hits the floor and returns a surface tag (StringName key, e.g. `&"stone"`)
- Default surface tag when no metadata present is `&"default"`
- Surface tag is read deterministically (same position = same result)
**Edge cases**: foot in air (no hit), multiple overlapping colliders, surface metadata missing
**Estimated test count**: ~4

### FS-004 Signal emission + integration (`footstep_emitted`) (Integration)
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/footstep_emitted_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `footstep_emitted` signal payload includes (surface_tag: StringName, noise_radius_m: float)
- `noise_radius_m` matches `PlayerCharacter.get_noise_level()` at emission time (the ADR-0002 contract)
- The signal fires through `Events`, never directly between sibling nodes
- A subscriber can connect and receive emissions reliably across SPRINT→WALK→CROUCH transitions
- Forbidden pattern `sai_subscribing_to_player_footstep` is registered (StealthAI must NOT subscribe to this signal — the registry assertion lives here even though Stealth AI is out-of-scope this sprint)
**Edge cases**: surface tag changes mid-walk (concrete to grass); player teleported (surface raycast updates the next frame)
**Estimated test count**: ~6

---

## Manual QA Checklist

The Sprint 02 manual checks are minimal — most stories are Logic and don't require manual verification beyond the smoke test. Items below are flagged because they have a "feel" or visual component that automated tests cannot catch.

### PC-002 First-person camera + look input — Manual feel-check
**Verification**: walk camera in stub Plaza scene
**Who signs off**: lead-programmer (proxy for design lead in solo mode)
**Evidence**: 1-minute screen recording in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-pc002-camera-look.md`
- [ ] Camera responds to mouse input with no perceived lag at 60 fps
- [ ] Pitch clamp at ±89° feels firm, not jittery
- [ ] Yaw is smooth across a 360° rotation; no roll bleeds in
- [ ] Sensitivity is in a usable range out-of-box (not so fast it's nauseating, not so slow it's frustrating)

### PC-003 Movement state machine — Manual feel-check
**Verification**: WASD + crouch + sprint walk-around in stub Plaza
**Who signs off**: lead-programmer (proxy for design lead in solo mode)
**Evidence**: 1-minute screen recording in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-pc003-movement-feel.md`
- [ ] Walk speed feels appropriate to a stealth game (deliberate, not slow)
- [ ] Sprint reads as faster, but isn't twitchy
- [ ] Crouch is visibly slower; the camera lowers proportionally
- [ ] Direction changes are responsive without sliding (Plaza has hard floors — no momentum slide expected)

### LS-002 State machine — Manual smoke
**Verification**: trigger forward swap into a stub Plaza section
**Who signs off**: lead-programmer
**Evidence**: log output captured in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-ls002-swap-log.md`
- [ ] Fade-out begins, fade-in ends; no flash-through
- [ ] `section_entered` signal payload's `TransitionReason` matches the call site
- [ ] No leftover nodes from previous section after swap
- [ ] LSS state returns to IDLE after swap completes

### PC-005 Interact raycast — Manual smoke
**Verification**: raycast hits a placeholder StaticBody3D
**Who signs off**: lead-programmer
**Evidence**: 1-screenshot in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-pc005-raycast.md`
- [ ] Raycast cursor (debug visualization) lands on intended target
- [ ] `get_current_interact_target()` returns the same node the raycast visualizes
- [ ] Pressing the interact action with target = null does not crash

### FS-004 Signal emission integration — Manual smoke
**Verification**: walk in stub Plaza with `EventLogger` attached
**Who signs off**: lead-programmer
**Evidence**: log output in `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-fs004-footstep-log.md`
- [ ] `footstep_emitted` fires at WALK cadence
- [ ] `footstep_emitted.noise_radius_m` payload value matches `PlayerCharacter.get_noise_level()` at emission time
- [ ] Sprint cadence is visibly faster; crouch cadence visibly slower
- [ ] No double-fire on direction reversal

---

## Smoke Test Scope

Run via `/smoke-check sprint`. Critical paths to verify before any QA hand-off this sprint:

1. **Game launches** without script errors or autoload failures (regression-critical: 5 new autoloads land this sprint)
2. **Stub Plaza scene loads** via `LevelStreamingService.transition_to_section("plaza_stub")` and renders
3. **Player walks** with WASD + look in stub Plaza; no movement-system errors
4. **Footstep signal** fires at expected cadence; `EventLogger` shows `footstep_emitted` with surface tag
5. **F5 quicksave** writes a valid `slot_0.res` (verify file exists post-save; Sprint 02 does not include UI feedback yet — Should-Have SL-005)
6. **Quit + relaunch + F9 quickload** restores Eve to the saved position; no state corruption
7. **No frame-rate regressions** — stub Plaza maintains 60 fps on the target Linux Vulkan setup
8. **GdUnit4 test suite** runs green: `godot -s -d res://addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd -a tests/unit -a tests/integration` exits 0
9. **No autoload-load-order errors** — all autoloads boot in the ADR-0007 documented order without cross-autoload reference faults

---

## Playtest Requirements

**No playtest sessions required for Sprint 02.**

Sprint 02 ships scaffolding + locomotion + persistence — all measurable via automated tests + the developer-driven smoke check + the four manual feel-checks above. The first playtest gates against Sprint 03+ when guards, documents, and HUD land and the gameplay loop becomes player-facing.

The Production gate requires ≥3 playtest sessions per `production/project-stage-report.md` — those will be scoped into Sprint 04 / Sprint 05.

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 02 (per-story)

A Sprint 02 story is **DONE** when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — via the automated test result OR the manual evidence document referenced above
- [ ] Test file exists at the path specified in the story's "Test Evidence Plan" section, AND tests pass when run via `godot -s -d res://addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd -a tests/unit -a tests/integration`
- [ ] For Visual/Feel and UI manual checks: evidence file exists at `production/qa/evidence/sprint-02-[story-id]-[slug].md` with checklist items confirmed
- [ ] No regressions surfaced by the smoke check
- [ ] `/code-review` ran on the implementation files (or a documented self-review for solo work)
- [ ] Story's `Status` field updated to `Complete` via `/story-done`
- [ ] `production/sprint-status.yaml` reflects the story as `status: done` with `completed: YYYY-MM-DD`

A Sprint 02 story is **BLOCKED** when:
- Any cross-epic dependency listed in `production/session-state/active.md` § "Known Cross-Epic Open Questions" surfaces during implementation. Most are out-of-scope for Sprint 02 — verify before marking blocked.
- An ADR-Proposed gate the story depends on is reached (none in Sprint 02 Must-Have selection).

---

## Notes

- **GdUnit4 4.6.2 patch**: tests will fail with a parse error if the patch at `addons/gdUnit4/src/core/GdUnitFileAccess.gd:199` is missing. Verify on every fresh clone with `grep -n "PATCH 2026-04-30" addons/gdUnit4/src/core/GdUnitFileAccess.gd`.
- **Test directory layout** mirrors source layout (`tests/unit/foundation/`, `tests/unit/core/`, `tests/integration/foundation/`, `tests/integration/core/`). Create subdirectories as needed when story tests land.
- **Naming convention**: `[system]_[scenario]_test.gd` for files; `test_[scenario]_[expected]` for functions (per `.claude/docs/coding-standards.md`).
- **Solo-mode QA**: this plan is the dev-and-the-tests; there is no separate QA pass. The dev runs the test suite and the smoke check before marking the story done.
